metar.name: Ozark/Cairns AAF, AL, US
metar.rawOb: SPECI KOZR 160515Z AUTO 16008KT 10SM OVC018 21/17 A2976 RMK AO2 SLP077 $

sevenDay.synopsis: Today, tomorrow, and most of Sunday look VFR and hazard-free. Another frontal boundary will approach the area late Sunday, sparking showers and ISOLD TS overnight, and IFR cigs will also set up. By Monday AM, the front is upon us, and we'll have to contend with cigs, gusty winds (G30kts), TURBC, showers, and Moderate TS (G35kts). We'll be monitoring for the possibility of severe weather, too. The front makes a quick exit late-AM but leaves behind gusty winds (G30kts) and TURBC for the PM. Winds should calm down by the start of the N1. Beyond, it's looking like a pretty quiet week with no sig weather expected thru the end of the forecast period.
sevenDay.title: 7-DAY OUTLOOK: 13 MAR - 19 MAR
